fact: April 26 is exactly 116 days into the year - that's almost one-third of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby animals to be born because the weather is getting warmer and food is becoming more abundant!
Cherry blossom petals are often falling like pink snow in many temperate regions right now, creating magical carpets on the ground.
The sun is rising about 2 minutes earlier each day around this time in most places - you can actually notice the difference!
If you're a Taurus, this date falls right in the middle of your zodiac season when the bull is supposedly at its strongest.
Dandelions are usually at their peak everywhere right now - those fluffy seed heads you blow on are perfect for making wishes!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are typically turning brilliant orange and red as autumn kicks into high gear.
Migrating butterflies like monarchs are often traveling thousands of miles right now in North America, and many pass through during this time each year.
Tree sap is flowing strongly in maple trees around this time in northern climates - you could literally tap one and taste nature's candy!
We're still 56 days away from the summer solstice, which means daylight is growing by about 2-3 minutes every single day in the Northern Hemisphere.
Earthworms are super active in the soil right now because the ground temperature is usually perfect for them to wiggle around and make dirt!
